author: Shaw
description: Export rendered Markdown tables in Obsidian to PNG, CSV, Excel, and PDF. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: shaw
manifest.description: Export Markdown tables and Bases views to PNG, CSV, Excel, and PDF — including virtualized Bases tables.
manifest.name: Table Exporter

name: Table Exporter

releases.author: wikty
releases.description:
### Added

- Obsidian `Bases` view support. `Bases Table` views can now be exported to `PNG`, `CSV`, `Excel (.xlsx)`, and `PDF` alongside Markdown tables.
- Scroll-walking collector for virtualized `Bases Table` rows. The plugin walks the scroll container, deduplicates rows by content fingerprint, and restores the original scroll position when done, so a 100-row `Bases Table` exports as 100 rows instead of only the currently visible window.
- Type-aware `Excel` and `CSV` output for `Bases` columns: `checkbox` columns become real boolean cells, `date` columns become ISO date strings, `number` columns are written as numeric cells, and link cells export readable text instead of raw control HTML.
- Faithful `PNG` and `PDF` rendering for Markdown tables whose cells contain `callout`, `Mermaid`, or `Dataview` blocks. The export pipeline detects complex rendered content and routes through DOM capture instead of the simplified SVG matrix renderer.
- New commands: `Export Bases view as PNG` and `Export Bases view as PDF with default options`. Existing `Export Markdown table as ...` commands continue to work and now also accept `Bases Table` views as the export source.
- Experimental visible-area `PNG` export for `Bases Cards`, `List`, and `Map` views. A runtime notice is shown at export time so users understand the limitation.
- `{{view}}` placeholder for the filename template, expanded from the source view's title.

### Changed

- Generalized internal table extraction to operate on any `HTMLElement` rather than only `HTMLTableElement`. Markdown table behavior is preserved; the change enables the `Bases` view code paths.
- `Excel` worksheet name is now derived from the view title (sanitized to 31 characters) instead of a hardcoded `Table`.
- Plugin notices and dialog labels now describe the source view kind (`Markdown table`, `Bases table`, `Bases cards`, etc.) instead of always saying `table`.
- Migrated from the deprecated `app.workspace.activeLeaf` API to `getActiveViewOfType` with a `getMostRecentLeaf` fallback, so the plugin is compatible with `Bases` view leaves in addition to `Markdown` views.
- `manifest.json` description and `package.json` keywords now explicitly mention `Bases`, so the plugin is discoverable by users searching for Bases-related export tools.

### Notes

- `Bases Cards`, `List`, and `Map` views currently export only the currently rendered viewport. `Bases` uses virtualized DOM rendering with element recycling for these view types, so unlike the `Table` view (which collects rows by scroll-walking), the visual views cannot yet produce a complete long-image without canvas stitching. Full long-image export for `Cards` is planned for `v0.3`.
- `CSV` and `Excel` exports remain matrix-based and are only available for `Markdown table` and `Bases Table` sources. Attempting `CSV` / `Excel` export from a `Cards` / `List` / `Map` view produces a clear error message rather than a malformed file.
- Upgrade from `0.1.x` is non-breaking. Existing `Markdown table` export behavior, commands, and settings are preserved. New `Bases` capabilities activate automatically when a `Bases` view is the active leaf.
- Internal test coverage for `Bases` DOM extraction is now backed by HTML fixtures under `tests/fixtures/` to detect regression if Obsidian's `Bases` DOM structure changes in future releases.
releases.name: Table Exporter 0.2.0

releases.name: Obsidian Table Exporter 0.1.1

releases.author: wikty
releases.description:
# Obsidian Table Exporter v0.1.0

First public release of `Obsidian Table Exporter`.

## What it does

- Export rendered Markdown tables in Obsidian as `PNG`
- Export rendered Markdown tables as paginated `PDF`
- Export table data as `CSV`
- Export table data as `Excel (.xlsx)`

## Highlights

- Works from the rendered table in the active note
- Prefers the table you most recently hovered or clicked
- Includes a `Clean export` visual mode for more shareable PNG/PDF output
- Supports per-export options for scale, background, PDF layout, and post-export actions
- Can copy exported PNG files directly to the clipboard

## Notes

- `PDF` export is currently image-based, so text in exported PDFs is not selectable yet
- The plugin is designed for rendered tables in reading view / preview workflows

## Included release asset

- `table-exporter-0.1.0.zip`
releases.name: Obsidian Table Exporter v0.1.0
